note: Reference dataLayer payloads for every event in the Task 1 GTM schema. requires_manual_push = true means the front-end dev must call window.dataLayer.push() explicitly (GTM has no native trigger for it). false means GTM's built-in trigger (Click, Form Submission, Scroll Depth) can read the event without a custom push, and the object shown is what GTM auto-generates or what the GTM tag maps from existing page/click variables.

events.trigger_type: Custom Event (GTM listens for this exact event name)
events.fires_when: User completes clinic + specialty selection and clicks Next
User enters contact details and clicks Next (no raw PII pushed)
Backend confirms the booking was actually created (not on button click)

events.trigger_type: GTM built-in Click Trigger, filtered on href starting with tel: or data-cta="call-now"
events.fires_when: User taps any Call Now button (homepage, clinic pages, landing page)
events.implementation_note: No manual push needed — GTM's Click variables ({{Click Text}}, {{Click URL}}) populate this directly. clinic_location comes from a page-level dataLayer variable set once on load, not per click.

events.trigger_type: GTM built-in Click Trigger, filtered on href containing wa.me
events.fires_when: User taps the floating WhatsApp widget
events.implementation_note: Same as call_now_click — built-in Click Trigger, no custom push required.

events.fires_when: User submits the name + phone gate form for the PDF guide
events.implementation_note: This is a real single-step <form> submit, so GTM's native Form Submission trigger works without any custom code.

events.fires_when: The PDF link is actually served after the gate form succeeds — not on click, so failed form submissions never get credited as a download

events.trigger_type: GTM Page View trigger (condition: Page Path matches /clinics/*), enriched with a page-level dataLayer object
events.fires_when: Any of the 9 clinic location pages loads
events.implementation_note: One trigger handles all 9 pages — clinic_name/clinic_city are set once as a page-level dataLayer object rendered server-side per page, not 9 separate GTM triggers.

events.fires_when: Reader scrolls past each threshold on a blog article
events.implementation_note: GTM auto-fires its own gtm.scrollDepth event with built-in variables — no manual dataLayer.push() needed. The GTM tag maps gtm.scrollDepth to a GA4 event named blog_scroll_depth so it reads cleanly in reporting.

events.fires_when: Landing page form (Task 2) passes client-side validation and submits — this is the campaign landing page's own 3-field form, separate from the main site's booking form above
events.implementation_note: Matches the live implementation in task2-landing-page/index.html. clinic_preference was added when the form gained a clinic dropdown, so this event now feeds both GA4 and the HubSpot integration in Task 3.
